mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering sharp negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter as corporate earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, which compresses corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day returns in >58% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is flat.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with slowing nonfarm payroll growth creates stagflationary pressure that forces equity repricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling PAYEMS yields positive 20-day returns in >62% of instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction triggers immediate recession fears, causing institutional portfolio rebalancing away from equities and generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 shows negative growth, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL correlates with positive 60-day returns in >65% of monthly observations, or the monthly Rank IC drops below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate alongside expanding industrial production indicates productivity-driven labor reallocation rather than demand collapse, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of rising UNRATE and rising INDPRO yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive significance in walk-forward tests.
